pattern: Keep a running left-hand total as you sweep the array once; the right-hand total for any position is simply the whole-array sum minus that running total minus the current element, so both sides can be compared without ever rescanning.
hints: The sum of everything after index i equals the total sum minus the sum of everything up to and including index i.
Precompute the total sum once, then walk the array left to right keeping a running sum of everything strictly before the current index.
At each index compare the running left sum against (total - leftSum - currentValue); the first index where they match is the answer, and if none match return -1.

edge_inputs.description: single element is trivially its own middle
all zeros makes every index a candidate, first one wins
negative and positive values balancing at the last index
middle index is the very first position

stress.description: random arrays near the max length
